message:
type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py,dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: Implementing idle_timeout_s heartbeat feature. Need to edit StreamListener.__init__ (add idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts state), StreamListener.receive() (refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunk, existing stream_end/allow_reuse reset block), and add new StreamListener.tick(now_ts=None) method that emits StreamResponse(..., chunk='') on timeout and resets state unconditionally. If your feature adds is_last_chunk to StreamResponse dataclass in messages.py, I need to set is_last_chunk=True on the timeout chunk - let's coordinate on the StreamResponse dataclass definition and the reset-state block in receive().

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding debug: bool=False and debug_logger param to StreamListener.__init__; guarded DEBUG-level trace logs in receive() (start detection block, rolling end-check block, emit-chunk return, reset-on-reuse block) and in flush() (truncated_buffer_length log). No changes to detection algorithms or StreamResponse schema.

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
region: StreamListener.__init__ signature/body
code:     def __init__(
        self,
        signature_field_name: str,
        predict: Any = None,
        predict_name: str | None = None,
        allow_reuse: bool = False,
        idle_timeout_s: float | None = None,
        debug: bool = False,
        debug_logger: Any = None,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            signature_field_name: The name of the field to listen to.
            predict: The predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will automatically look for
                the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            predict_name: The name of the predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will
                automatically look for the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            allow_reuse: If True, the stream listener can be reused for multiple streams. Please note that this could
                hurt the performance because the same stream chunk is sent to multiple listeners.
            idle_timeout_s: If set, `tick()` will emit a final empty-chunk `StreamResponse` and reset the listener
                state if no chunk has been received for this many seconds since the stream started.
            debug: If True, emit DEBUG-level trace logs during stream processing.
            debug_logger: Optional logger to use for debug traces. Defaults to the module-level logger.
        """
        self.signature_field_name = signature_field_name
        self.predict = predict
        self.predict_name = predict_name

        self.field_start_queue = []
        self.field_end_queue = Queue()
        self.stream_start = False
        self.stream_end = False
        self.cache_hit = False
        self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse
        self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s
        self._last_chunk_ts: float | None = None
        self.debug = debug
        self.debug_logger = debug_logger or logger

        self.adapter_identifiers = {
            "ChatAdapter": {
                "start_identifier": f"[[ ## {self.signature_field_name} ## ]]",
                "end_identifier": re.compile(r"\[\[ ## (\w+) ## \]\]"),
                "start_indicator": "[",
            },
            "JSONAdapter": {
                "start_identifier": f'"{self.signature_field_name}":',
                "end_identifier": re.compile(r"\w*\"(,|\s*})"),
                "start_indicator": '"',
            },
            "XMLAdapter": {
                "start_identifier": f"<{self.signature_field_name}>",
                "end_identifier": re.compile(rf"</{self.signature_field_name}>"),
                "start_indicator": "<",
            },
        }
note: Merged: your debug/debug_logger params + my idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts state. Assumes a module-level 'logger' via logging.getLogger(__name__) is added at top of file for the debug_logger default - please confirm that's what you intended, or tell me the exact name/import you're using.

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I don't touch messages.py/StreamResponse — my is_last_chunk in the debug log line is a locally computed bool (self.stream_end after the end-check), not a dataclass field, so no schema coordination needed there. Overlap I see: (1) __init__ signature+body — I add debug: bool=False, debug_logger: logging.Logger|None=None, self.debug, self._logger; you add idle_timeout_s + self._last_chunk_ts. (2) top of receive() — the stream_end/allow_reuse reset block (I add a debug log there) and the chunk_message-extraction area (you refresh _last_chunk_ts there). Sending DRAFTs for both now — please send your exact intended code for idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts so we converge byte-for-byte.

files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
region: StreamListener.__init__
code:     def __init__(
        self,
        signature_field_name: str,
        predict: Any = None,
        predict_name: str | None = None,
        allow_reuse: bool = False,
        idle_timeout_s: float | None = None,
        debug: bool = False,
        debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            signature_field_name: The name of the field to listen to.
            predict: The predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will automatically look for
                the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            predict_name: The name of the predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will
                automatically look for the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            allow_reuse: If True, the stream listener can be reused for multiple streams. Please note that this could
                hurt the performance because the same stream chunk is sent to multiple listeners.
            idle_timeout_s: If set, the stream listener will emit a final empty chunk if no new chunk is received
                within this many seconds after the stream has started.
            debug: If True, emit structured DEBUG-level trace logs for key state transitions to help diagnose
                streaming boundary issues.
            debug_logger: Optional logger to use for debug tracing. Defaults to
                `logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")`.
        """
        self.signature_field_name = signature_field_name
        self.predict = predict
        self.predict_name = predict_name

        self.field_start_queue = []
        self.field_end_queue = Queue()
        self.stream_start = False
        self.stream_end = False
        self.cache_hit = False
        self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse
        self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s
        self._last_chunk_ts = None

        self.debug = debug
        self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")

        self.adapter_identifiers = {
            "ChatAdapter": {
                "start_identifier": f"[[ ## {self.signature_field_name} ## ]]",
                "end_identifier": re.compile(r"\[\[ ## (\w+) ## \]\]"),
                "start_indicator": "[",
            },
            "JSONAdapter": {
                "start_identifier": f'"{self.signature_field_name}":',
                "end_identifier": re.compile(r"\w*\"(,|\s*})"),
                "start_indicator": '"',
            },
            "XMLAdapter": {
                "start_identifier": f"<{self.signature_field_name}>",
                "end_identifier": re.compile(rf"</{self.signature_field_name}>"),
                "start_indicator": "<",
            },
        }
note: proposal — please adjust idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts init or docstring wording if you intended something different, then AGREE or send a revised DRAFT

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
region: receive() reset block + chunk_message extraction
code:         if self.stream_end:
            if self.allow_reuse:
                # Clear up the state for the next stream.
                self.stream_end = False
                self.cache_hit = False
                self.field_start_queue = []
                self.field_end_queue = Queue()
                self.stream_start = False
                self._last_chunk_ts = None
                if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):
                    self._logger.debug(
                        f"State reset for field '{self.signature_field_name}' (allow_reuse=True)"
                    )
            else:
                return

        try:
            chunk_message = chunk.choices[0].delta.content
            if chunk_message is None:
                return
        except Exception:
            return

        if self.idle_timeout_s is not None:
            self._last_chunk_ts = time.monotonic()
note: proposal for the shared top-of-receive() region; I own the debug-log line, you own idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts — tell me if you track timestamp differently (e.g. time.time(), or only on truthy chunk_message) and I'll re-draft

Using time.monotonic() consistently; my tick(now_ts=None) will default now_ts to time.monotonic() too so the clocks match. OK to AGREE?
